title: Recent Talks
text:
Only talks from the past 12 months are listed here. Older talks are available in the Archive.

2026-03 — The 73rd Annual Meeting of the Ecological Society of Japan (Kyoto)
K. Kakinuma, A. Yanagawa, and H. Kim, “Ecological Resistance to Drought and Child Malnutrition: Linking Ecosystem Function to Human Health”
2025-10 — The 4th Asian Population Forum (Shanghai)
K. Kakinuma, “Linking spatially uneven population aging to heatstroke mortality in Japan (1995–2022)”
2025-07 — International Population Conference 2025 (Brisbane)
K. Kakinuma and N. Inoue, “Climate Change and an Aging Society: The Rapid Increase of Heatstroke Deaths in Japan (1972–2022)”

title: Members

準備中

title: Kakinuma Lab.
text:
Kakinuma Lab.


Frontier Research Institute for Interdisciplinary Sciences, Tohoku University




Societal impacts of climate change from local to global
Our lab focuses on the interplay between climate and environmental change and population dynamics, examining how climate change affects human societies across multiple spatial scales. We empirically investigate its impacts on human migration, health, ecosystem vulnerability, and social inequality. Our goal is to deepen understanding of these patterns and mechanisms from local to global levels and to support the development of adaptation strategies for future climate extremes.

ORCID · Scholar · Researchmap · Instagram · Bluesky




気候変動の社会影響を、地域から全球まで横断的に解明する
本研究室では、気候・環境変化と人口動態の相互作用に着目し、気候変動が人間社会に与える影響を地域から全球までの多様な空間スケールで明らかにします。とくに、人口移動、健康、生態系の脆弱性、社会的格差への影響を実証的に検証しています。地域から全球までのパターンとメカニズムの理解を深め、気候変動適応策の検討につなげることを目標としています。
